candidates.title: Aesop's Fables (George Fyler Townsend translation)
candidates.quality_notes: Classic translation, prose style, widely available
candidates.confidence_notes: Project Gutenberg text is clean and well-structured. Numbering may not match Chambry exactly.
candidates.ranking_rationale: Well-known PD translation, clean text, good for alignment if fable can be identified
candidates.alignment_fitness: moderate - prose style may not match Greek verse structure perfectly

candidates.title: Aesop's Fables (V.S. Vernon Jones translation)
candidates.confidence_notes: Available on Gutenberg. May have different fable selection than Chambry.
candidates.ranking_rationale: More modern language than Townsend, good readability
candidates.alignment_fitness: moderate - prose translation, structure may vary

candidates.title: Fables of Aesop and Other Eminent Mythologists (Roger L'Estrange)
candidates.quality_notes: Very early translation, archaic language, includes moral commentary
candidates.confidence_notes: Historical interest but challenging language for modern readers
candidates.ranking_rationale: Too archaic for primary witness, possible auxiliary historical reference
candidates.alignment_fitness: low - very different structure and style

candidates.title: Fables of Aesop (Samuel Croxall)
candidates.ranking_rationale: Historical translation, useful as secondary witness
candidates.alignment_fitness: moderate - structured but may expand on original

candidates.title: Aesop's Fables - Wikisource (English)
candidates.confidence_notes: Good for finding additional PD translations, variable structure
candidates.ranking_rationale: Useful index but not primary source
